head: a801f38

index.name: docs_tv_4bit
index.note: Phase P pre-bakes blocked codes + codebook into the relfile, ~2x the v1.2.0 size (793 MiB) but eliminates per-backend prep cost

comparison.warm_p50_phase_p: not measured (warm sweep script bug; warm path unchanged)

configs.label: tv_4bit_k100_cold_phase_p
configs.method: 50 fresh psql sessions, each issues one EXPLAIN ANALYZE LIMIT 10 query

verdict_reasoning: 21x speedup vs Phase O-2 baseline confirms the per-backend pack::repack + Lloyd-Max codebook were the dominant cold-scan costs.

1.26 s cold p50 is acceptable for a fresh-backend first-query on a 1 M x 1536-d corpus; HNSW's ~100 ms cold-equivalent on this corpus benefits from PG's shared_buffers preserving across backends, which our path now uses too.
Subsequent queries in the same backend hit the warm cache at ~87 ms (Phase O-2 baseline; Phase P doesn't change the warm path).
1.5 GB on-disk index is 2x the v1.2.0 size (793 MiB) but still 5x smaller than pgvector HNSW's 8 192 MB on the same corpus.
Phase Q can now safely flip relfile_storage default ON and remove the side-table path.
